docx: Module 41 · Unit 4 · Activity 1 — Proposal for Instructional Coaching
School / District Working with: Victoria Thang Long — Independent 1:1 Chromebook campus · Grades 4–9 · ~540 students · 18 Math & Science teachers
Primary Coaching Frame: Jim Knight's Instructional Coaching Cycle (Identify → Learn → Improve) anchored by GROW for goal-setting and TPACK/SAMR for technology fit

These recommendations are drawn directly from administrator free-response answers on the Module 41 survey and from teacher self-identified coaching requests. Each coaching plan names a coaching model, a measurable starting goal, and a five-meeting arc (exceeding the template's minimum of three) across an 8-week coaching cycle.
Rough Schedule of Meetings and Objectives (5 scheduled — exceeds minimum of 3)
Rough Schedule of Meetings and Objectives (5 scheduled — exceeds minimum of 3)
Victor Moronu  |  MTEC 02/2026 Cohort 2  |  Instructor: Charles Guilford  |  vmoronu@gmail.com

xlsx.Unnamed: 9: Strengthen data-driven instruction. Ms. Vectore uses IXL daily but her dashboard-to-grouping pipeline is unclear. Also targeting off-task tab behavior during independent practice — 3 walkthroughs in October showed 4–6 students on YouTube/gaming tabs.
xlsx.Unnamed: 11: Mr. Luis Luma
xlsx.Unnamed: 12: Push beyond read-and-quiz to interactive simulations and EdPuzzle. Mr. Luma is a content expert and student-favorite — his Chromebook time should match that strength. Also would like to see him use Classroom announcements so schoolwide comms land.
xlsx.Unnamed: 14: Improved academic outcomes (MAP, ERB); Increased teacher confidence; Improved assessment data use; Consistent technology use
xlsx.Unnamed: 15: Every classroom has one device-management routine everyone can name; teachers pull at least one instructional move per week from their data; walkthrough look-fors show tech used for creation, not just consumption; new-teacher onboarding includes a tech playbook.

xlsx.Unnamed: 7: Data-driven instruction; Student engagement strategies; Creating interactive lessons
xlsx.Unnamed: 9: Grow station-rotation facility in her 8th-grade Algebra sections. She has the kids and the curriculum — needs a structured rotation routine.
xlsx.Unnamed: 11: Mr. Karim El-Sayed
xlsx.Unnamed: 12: Desmos deep-dive. Mr. El-Sayed is already strong in Math but underuses Desmos Activity Builder; unlocking this tool could model for the whole Math department.
xlsx.Unnamed: 14: Improved academic outcomes (MAP, ERB); Increased teacher confidence; Improved assessment data use

xlsx.Unnamed: 9: Mr. Patel's 7th-grade Science sections have the highest device-write-up rate in Q1 — rarely behavior-motivated, almost always management-design. Needs support building predictable routines.
xlsx.Unnamed: 11: Ms. Ada Ijeoma
xlsx.Unnamed: 12: Ms. Ijeoma is excellent with kids but Chromebook time in her 6th-grade Science class often drifts to YouTube. Needs GoGuardian training and a clear transition protocol.
xlsx.Unnamed: 14: Consistent technology use; Increased teacher confidence; Increased student engagement
xlsx.Unnamed: 15: Zero-surprise expectations: kids, teachers, and admin all know what Chromebook time looks like and sounds like.
